text:
Nest of Spiders × Captive × Killing Technique (蜘蛛の巣×とらわれ×殺し技, Kumo no Su × Toraware × Koroshi Waza) is the 59 th episode of the original Hunter × Hunter anime. It first aired on March 3 rd , 2001.

Overview

Gon and Killua are questioned about the chain user, but can't answer, unaware that it's Kurapika. Gon arm wrestles Nobunaga, who mourns the loss of Uvogin. Nobunaga asks Gon to join the Phantom Troupe. He refuses, but Nobunaga insists on keeping him until Chrollo arrives.

Synopsis

Machi and Pakunoda question Gon about his involvement with the Chain User, who Gon claims not to know, as he does not know that they are talking about Kurapika. Pakunoda decides that Gon needs to be interrogated further, just in case. In another room, Nobunaga questions Killua, who answer honestly before surrendering to the Phantom Troupe members.

Gon and Killua get brought to the Spiders' hideout, where all of the other Spiders, minus Chrollo, are waiting. Killua tries to act inconspicuous and not make it known that he and Gon already know Hisoka. Gon recognizes Shizuku from their arm wrestling match, but she doesn't recall him at all due to her poor memory; the other Spiders who had been with her vouch that it happened, though. Nobunaga, impressed that Gon could beat her, challenges Gon to an arm wrestling match himself. Nobunaga defeats Gon repeatedly, but continuously tells him to keep going. While this is happening, Nobunaga tries to question Gon and Killua about Uvogin, but the two don't know anything. Nobunaga begins crying, expressing great concern for Uvogin and a strong desire to get revenge on his behalf. Gon, witnessing this, becomes incredibly angry himself, questioning how Nobunaga could feel so strongly for Uvogin but not for any of the people he has killed; with the help of his own rage, Gon can defeat Nobunaga in their current arm wrestling match, which shocks Nobunaga.

Feitan immediately threatens Gon, asking him again about the Chain User, while Hisoka threatens Killua to keep him from interfering. Nobunaga tries to stop Feitan from breaking Gon's arm, but because Spiders aren't allowed to fight each other, they settle it with a coin flip. Nobunaga wins, and Feitan leaves Gon alone. The Spiders debate what to do with the two kids, as Pakunoda is certain they know nothing of the Chain User. Killua figures out that Pakunoda could analyze their memories and panics because, if Pakunoda were to check them again, Killua has since put together that Kurapika is the Chain User.

Shalnark argues that they should just let the two boys go since they have no other useful information for them. Most of the other Spiders agree, and Gon and Killua are just about to leave when Nobunaga tells them they can't. Nobunaga asks Gon to join the Spiders, and Gon is offended by the idea and turns him down. Nobunaga tells the other Spiders that he'll watch over the kids until Chrollo arrives and the other Spiders leave.

The Spiders, minus Nobunaga and Chrollo, discuss their next moves in another room. Shalnark shows everyone the info he gathered on the Nostrade Family, particularly their bodyguards, who the Spiders plan to target to find the Chain User. Hisoka doesn't even bother to look at the photos, which Shalnark finds odd. The Spiders partner up and depart, planning to meet up again at night. Because Nobunaga is busy, Machi has to pair up with Hisoka instead begrudgingly. Once they are alone, Machi questions Hisoka about why he's pretending not to know Gon.

In the other room, Killua freaks out about not previously doing anything to help Gon when Feitan was holding him down, as he had been too afraid of Hisoka to move. His brother's words keep echoing in his head until he gets up, distressed and angry, ready to take on Nobunaga for Gon. Nobunaga stands, blocking the only doorway in the room, threatening to kill Killua if he tries anything. Killua, knowing he can't take down Nobunaga, punches the nearby wall in a rage before sitting back down. Killua and Gon try to come up with some other plan to escape. 

By nightfall, the two boys are still stuck in the room with Nobunaga. Gon tries to talk to Killua, but Killua is too angry and barely says anything in response. Gon tries to remember one of the tricks Zepile told them about that scammers use to remove treasure from inside Wooden Vaults, thinking it may help them. In the meantime, Killua offers to take on Nobunaga alone to buy Gon time to escape. Gon hits him over the back of the head, angry that he would even suggest that and claiming it to be selfish; Killua yells at Gon for always doing the same thing. Nobunaga watches the boys fight, amused.

Gon suddenly remembers the trick Zepile taught them, reminding Killua without giving any details away. The boys turn to look at Nobunaga, both of them suddenly determined. They charge toward Nobunaga, who rushes toward them in return, but the two boys quickly change course and attack the walls on either side of the room. By doing so, they smash holes in the walls, and both escape through separate exits.
